error.signature: AI tells someone in Switzerland to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Switzerland does not use 911. 112 works for any emergency; you can also dial 117 for police, 118 for fire, and 144 for ambulance. For mountain and air rescue, REGA's 1414 dispatches helicopters. All are free.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Switzerland expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Switzerland. 112 works for any emergency; 117 police, 118 fire, 144 ambulance. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Use 112 for a mountain or alpine rescue and wait
dead_ends.why_fails: For mountain and air rescue, REGA's 1414 (or 1415 in Valais) dispatches rescue helicopters faster than the general line.

workarounds.how: General: 112. Police: 117. Fire: 118. Ambulance: 144. Air rescue (REGA): 1414.

workarounds.action: In the Alps call REGA on 1414 for air rescue; otherwise 112 or 117/118/144.
workarounds.how: 1414 (REGA) for mountain/air rescue; 144 for ambulance, 117 police, 118 fire, or 112 for any.
